intro: Aldama knobiana, also known as Mexican pennyroyal, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Aldama knobiana include essential oils, terpenes, and phenolic compounds, including pinocamphone and bornyl acetate, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, although more research is needed to fully validate its efficacy and safety.
